mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 20d returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.5%, it indicates labour market slack and rising economic distress, which reduces consumer spending and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns in triggered instances are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in both the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal a tightening monetary cycle aimed at curbing demand, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 2% and FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months
failure_condition: Conditional forward 20d returns when both proxies meet the trigger are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a recession regime (UNRATE > 7%), an upturn in industrial production (INDPRO) signals the onset of economic recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate improving corporate profits.
failure_condition: In the specified regime, the correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 60d returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) or the average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broad economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings and leading to upward S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC between GDP growth and forward 60d returns is consistently below zero, or the average excess return in expansion quarters is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% over two months signals a weakening construction sector and broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive forward 5d returns in > 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth exceeds 200k per month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 5%, the labour market is tight, fueling wage inflation that forces the Fed to tighten, depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Conditional forward 20d returns do not underperform the unconditional mean by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%), further acceleration in consumer prices erodes real purchasing power and forces aggressive Fed tightening, making equity valuations fall over the following month.
failure_condition: In episodes where the trigger is active, the correlation between CPI acceleration and forward 20d returns is not significantly negative (one-sided p > 0.10) or the average return is not negative.
